title: Wiborgiella leipoldtiana
intro: Wiborgiella leipoldtiana, commonly known as Wiborg's cycad, is a species of plant in the family Stangeriaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Bioactive constituents of Wiborgiella leipoldtiana include cycasin, a known toxin, as well as other compounds with potential medicinal properties such as flavonoids and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Wiborgiella leipoldtiana are supplements and infusions, although its use is often cautioned due to the potential toxicity of its cycasin content.
